subject: Sports
title: Usain Bolt's Lightning Bolt
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Usain Bolt's Lightning Bolt.

hook.text: A Jamaican sprinter ran so fast that he slowed down to celebrate—and still broke the world record.

buildup.text: At Beijing 2008, Usain Bolt entered the 100m final as a relative newcomer to the distance.

discovery.text: He ran 9.69 seconds—a world record—while visibly decelerating in the final 20 meters to celebrate.

twist.text: Scientists estimated he could have run 9.55 seconds if he'd sprinted through the line.

climax.text: Bolt won eight Olympic golds across three Games, earning the title of fastest human in history.

punchline.text: He celebrated before the finish line and still broke the record.

quiz.question: What was unusual about Bolt's world record run in Beijing?
options: He slowed down to celebrate before the finish line
He started from the wrong lane
